text: You have power over your mind — not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
short: You have power over your mind — not outside events.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts: therefore, guard accordingly, and take care that you entertain no notions unsuitable to virtue and reasonable nature.
short: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.
short: Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: When you arise in the morning, think of what a precious privilege it is to be alive — to breathe, to think, to enjoy, to love.
short: When you arise in the morning, think of what a privilege it is to be alive.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The best revenge is to be unlike him who performed the injury.
short: The best revenge is to be unlike him who performed the injury.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: It is not death that a man should fear, but he should fear never beginning to live.
short: It is not death that a man should fear, but never beginning to live.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Very little is needed to make a happy life; it is all within yourself, in your way of thinking.
short: Very little is needed to make a happy life; it is all within yourself.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The object of life is not to be on the side of the majority, but to escape finding oneself in the ranks of the insane.
short: The object of life is not to be on the side of the majority.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Accept the things to which fate binds you, and love the people with whom fate brings you together, and do so with all your heart.
short: Accept the things to which fate binds you, and love the people fate brings.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Never esteem anything as of advantage to you that will make you break your word or lose your self-respect.
short: Never esteem anything that will make you break your word.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The soul becomes dyed with the colour of its thoughts.
short: The soul becomes dyed with the colour of its thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: How much more grievous are the consequences of anger than the causes of it.
short: How much more grievous are the consequences of anger than the causes of it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: If it is not right do not do it; if it is not true do not say it.
short: If it is not right do not do it; if it is not true do not say it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Look well into thyself; there is a source of strength which will always spring up if thou wilt always look.
short: Look well into thyself; there is a source of strength which will always spring up.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Dwell on the beauty of life. Watch the stars, and see yourself running with them.
short: Dwell on the beauty of life. Watch the stars, and see yourself running with them.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Everything we hear is an opinion, not a fact. Everything we see is a perspective, not the truth.
short: Everything we hear is an opinion, not a fact.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Begin each day by telling yourself: today I shall be meeting with interference, ingratitude, insolence, disloyalty, ill-will, and selfishness.
short: Begin each day by telling yourself: today I shall meet interference and ingratitude.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Loss is nothing else but change, and change is nature's delight.
short: Loss is nothing else but change, and change is nature's delight.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Do not indulge in dreams of having what you have not, but reckon up the chief of the blessings you do possess, and then thankfully remember how you would crave them if they were not yours.
short: Do not dream of having what you have not, but reckon the blessings you possess.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: He who lives in harmony with himself lives in harmony with the universe.
short: He who lives in harmony with himself lives in harmony with the universe.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.
short: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Think of yourself as dead. You have lived your life. Now, take what's left and live it properly.
short: Think of yourself as dead. Now take what's left and live it properly.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: No man is free who is not master of himself.
short: No man is free who is not master of himself.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: To refrain from imitation is the best revenge.
short: To refrain from imitation is the best revenge.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: How much time he gains who does not look to see what his neighbour says or does or thinks, but only at what he does himself.
short: How much time he gains who does not look to see what his neighbour does.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Whenever you are about to find fault with someone, ask yourself the following question: What fault of mine most nearly resembles the one I am about to criticize?
short: Before finding fault with someone, ask what fault of yours most resembles theirs.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Be tolerant with others and strict with yourself.
short: Be tolerant with others and strict with yourself.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The things you think about determine the quality of your mind. Your soul takes on the color of your thoughts.
short: The things you think about determine the quality of your mind.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Do every act of your life as though it were the very last act of your life.
short: Do every act of your life as though it were the very last.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Here is a rule to remember in future, when anything tempts you to feel bitter: not 'This is misfortune,' but 'To bear this worthily is good fortune.'
short: Not 'This is misfortune,' but 'To bear this worthily is good fortune.'
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: You always own the option of having no opinion. There is never any need to get worked up or to trouble your soul about things you can't control.
short: You always own the option of having no opinion.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: If someone is able to show me that what I think or do is not right, I will happily change, for I seek the truth.
short: If someone shows me what I think is not right, I will happily change.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: It is in your power to withdraw yourself whenever you desire. Perfect tranquillity within consists in the good ordering of the mind.
short: It is in your power to withdraw yourself whenever you desire.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Reject your sense of injury and the injury itself disappears.
short: Reject your sense of injury and the injury itself disappears.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Our life is what our thoughts make it.
short: Our life is what our thoughts make it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The first rule is to keep an untroubled spirit. The second is to look things in the face and know them for what they are.
short: Keep an untroubled spirit. Look things in the face and know them for what they are.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Receive without conceit, release without struggle.
short: Receive without conceit, release without struggle.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Confine yourself to the present.
short: Confine yourself to the present.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Do not act as if you were going to live ten thousand years. Death hangs over you. While you live, while it is in your power, be good.
short: Do not act as if you will live ten thousand years. While you live, be good.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The art of living is more like wrestling than dancing, in so far as it stands ready against the accidental and the unforeseen.
short: The art of living is more like wrestling than dancing.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: What we do now echoes in eternity.
short: What we do now echoes in eternity.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: When you arise in the morning, think of what a precious privilege it is to be alive, to breathe, to think, to enjoy, to love.
short: When you arise, think of the precious privilege it is to be alive.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: A man's worth is no greater than the worth of his ambitions.
short: A man's worth is no greater than the worth of his ambitions.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Never value anything as profitable that compels you to break your promise, to lose your self-respect, to hate any man, to suspect, to curse.
short: Never value anything as profitable that compels you to break your promise.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: If you are distressed by anything external, the pain is not due to the thing itself, but to your estimate of it; and this you have the power to revoke at any moment.
short: If you are distressed by anything external, the pain is due to your estimate of it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The universe is change; our life is what our thoughts make it.
short: The universe is change; our life is what our thoughts make it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Nothing happens to any man that he is not formed by nature to bear.
short: Nothing happens to any man that he is not formed by nature to bear.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Adapt yourself to the things among which your lot has been cast and love sincerely the fellow creatures with whom destiny has ordained that you shall live.
short: Adapt yourself to the things among which your lot has been cast.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: You have power over your mind — not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
short: Realize your power is over your mind — not outside events.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Look back over the past, with its changing empires that rose and fell, and you can foresee the future too.
short: Look back over the past and you can foresee the future too.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: That which is not good for the swarm, neither is it good for the bee.
short: That which is not good for the swarm, neither is it good for the bee.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The happiness of those who want to be popular depends on others; the happiness of those who seek pleasure fluctuates with moods outside their control; but the happiness of the wise grows out of their own free acts.
short: The happiness of the wise grows out of their own free acts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Choose not to be harmed — and you won't feel harmed. Don't feel harmed — and you haven't been.
short: Choose not to be harmed — and you won't feel harmed.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Remember that very little is needed to make a happy life.
short: Remember that very little is needed to make a happy life.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: It is not things that disturb us, but our judgement about things.
short: It is not things that disturb us, but our judgement about things.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Execute every act of thy life as though it were thy last.
short: Execute every act of thy life as though it were thy last.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The only wealth which you will keep forever is the wealth you have given away.
short: The only wealth you will keep forever is the wealth you have given away.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Time is a sort of river of passing events, and strong is its current; no sooner is a thing brought to sight than it is swept by and another takes its place, and this too will be swept away.
short: Time is a river of passing events, and strong is its current.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The best answer to anger is silence.
short: The best answer to anger is silence.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Be like the cliff against which the waves continually break; but it stands firm and tames the fury of the water around it.
short: Be like the cliff against which the waves continually break; it stands firm.
author: Marcus Aurelius
